title: Static compilation using libpod - executable fails
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Trying to compile golang static binary using libpod. The command line flags to build go program are below -
 
```CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -tags containers_image_openpgp -ldflags '-w'```

Go code used is - 
```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"

	lp "github.com/containers/libpod/libpod"
)

func main() {

	r, _ := lp.NewRuntime(context.Background())
	lpp, _ := r.GetContainers()
	fmt.Println(len(lpp))
}
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a small go program listed above
2. Compile the go code using the compilation method above. Compilation would be successful and a binary is created.
3. Execute the binary


**Describe the results you received:**

The go executable results in the following error -

```
WARN[0000] Error initializing configured OCI runtime runc: no valid executable found for OCI runtime runc: invalid argument
WARN[0000] Error initializing configured OCI runtime kata: no valid executable found for OCI runtime kata: invalid argument
ERRO[0000] locks are not supported without cgo
ERRO[0000] locks are not supported without cgo
ERRO[0000] locks are not supported without cgo
ERRO[0000] Error retrieving container a4ac37320cb3176247121e76afb881f58bf83d28eb51fbbb6dfd63502bf0f9f7 from the database: error retrieving lock for container a4ac37320cb3176247121e76afb881f58bf83d28eb51fbbb6dfd63502bf0f9f7: lock ID 0 is too large - max lock size is 4294967295: invalid argument
0
``` 

The strace of the binary is provided as file strace.out


comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

Please note that the `libpod` package is not supported outside of Podman.  If you need bindings in Go, please use [pkg/bindings](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/pkg/bindings).

I think you can change the locks to use the file driver instead of shm.

But I agree with @vrothberg that this is not something we can support at the moment.
I think the file driver also uses CGo. Generally speaking, building a Podman binary that includes Libpod without CGo is not going to result in a usable executable.
